actionable_feedback: Add a full remediation-combination enumeration table in annotation_budget.md showing every combo considered, per-combo summed cost and aggregated impact (score delta or benefit), and the algorithm used to search combos (exhaustive, greedy, knapsack heuristic, etc.).
Explicitly document tie-breaking rules for boundary cases (e.g., equal cost, equal score, or combos that exactly meet budget) and show an example application of the tie-breaker.
Add a short checklist mapping each numeric CSV cell to the exact packet field (file and line or JSON path) used to compute it so reviewers can trace CSV cells to source packet values.
Include the full non-truncated contents of both artifacts (or append full contents in a supplementary file) so graders/verifiers can validate exhaustive claims; ensure previews show the new content.

Provide a tiny, runnable verification script or unit tests (e.g., node/python) that execute the provided JS scoring snippet over all rows and print expected outputs; include expected outputs in the artifact so graders can re-run deterministically.

Explicitly state and justify the chosen normalization family (min-max to packet maxima is currently used) and clarify the rationale for excluding or incorporating gain_pts_per100 in the score; if excluding, state that explicitly and why.

If exhaustive enumeration was performed, add a brief note on computational completeness (e.g., 'exhaustive over 2^N combos for N options' or 'heuristic used with coverage guarantee') and a runtime/perf note so verifiers can judge reproducibility.
feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables satisfy the artifact contract and rubric: per-row raw packet values, reproducible scoring formulas, sensitivity checks, remediation budget arithmetic, and a best-feasible remediation combo are present. The grader and verifier both assigned full credit. Remaining concerns are primarily about visibility and explicitness: previews were truncated so the claim of exhaustive remediation-combination enumeration cannot be fully validated; tie-breaking logic and a cell-to-packet-field checklist are not explicit in the visible previews; and the normalization family and treatment of gain_pts_per100 are acknowledged as ambiguous (correctly flagged needs_review).
